lines: A man is hammered to a chapel cross behind the altar, thrown into black silhouette by a tall window behind him. The window shows no sky, no day, no moon -- only a flat, sourceless light pouring in, and its lead came is shaped into an inverted cross.

He is not only a man anymore. Bone has bloomed into a broken halo behind his skull; black-gold runs beneath his skin like lit wire. The Choir has been slicing the closed flesh from his ribs and arms, and it keeps growing back for them to cut again.
His head turns the little the spikes allow. He looks down the length of the cross at you and whispers the same words, over and over: "Looking down the cross... speak no evil..."
You could end him. You could leave him to whatever the Choir intends. Either way, this is a thing you will carry. Choices on this road have a way of waiting for you further down it.

effects.log: You step back and leave him on the cross. His whisper follows you across the stone.
You drive the spike deep and end him. The light behind the cross gutters out. The whispering stops.

lines: The halo fades to dead bone. The carved body sags on the spikes and is, at last, only a body.
Mercy, or evidence burned, or one less saint for the Choir to finish: you will not know which you chose until later. On this road, you rarely do.
You leave him hung and half-living, still growing, still being cut from, still whispering down the length of the cross.

Perhaps the Compact would pay to take him alive. Perhaps the Remnant would burn the whole chapel for less. Either way, he is still here when you turn your back, and he will still be here when someone else comes.
